hero.tagline: An interactive architecture map generated from your code, curated by hand, and built to be presented to non-technical people. Hand-drawn diagrams go stale in three months; auto-generated dependency graphs are a 300-node hairball. codecarto is neither.

scan.title: Automatic analysis
scan.body: ts-morph scans import / export / dynamic import, plus a Next.js semantic layer: Pages, API routes (with HTTP methods), fetch('/api/…') wiring, service nodes like Prisma / Stripe, and 'use client' boundaries. Always in sync with the code.

curate.title: Human curation
curate.body: Rename nodes into business language ("Member login"), color, group, hide, draw manual edges. Curation lives in codecarto.map.json (commit it) and is re-applied by stable node IDs after every rescan — renaming a file won't lose your work.

present.title: Communication
present.body: Presentation mode walks a data flow step by step, NamedViews save your go-to perspectives, clicking highlights the up/downstream chain, and exports (PNG / SVG / interactive HTML) go straight to your client.

highlights.v: Generated from the code on every run — the map can't drift from reality.
Curate technical nodes into the language your stakeholders actually use.
Walk a flow, highlight a path, export a clean image — without screen-sharing an IDE.
Scanning runs on your machine; the only file written is codecarto.map.json.

home.demoLabel: Live Demo — the real UI; curation lives in memory only, play freely
home.ctaTitle: Map your project in one command
home.ctaBody: No config required. Point it at any TypeScript or Next.js project and open the map in your browser.

features.title: Features
features.intro: Everything codecarto does, grouped by the three layers — analysis, curation, communication — plus what keeps it fast on large graphs.

semantics.v: Pages / API routes / fetch wiring / service detection / 'use client' boundaries

services.v: Built-in rules for Prisma, Drizzle, Mongoose, Supabase, Stripe, Resend, AWS, Redis, Firebase, OpenAI, Anthropic — extend with your own

stable.v: Curation merges back by relative path / route, so a rescan never loses your edits

curation.v: Shape the map by hand; all stored in codecarto.map.json

manual.v: Draw logical connections the analyzer can't infer from static code

migration.v: When a file is renamed, stale curation is detected and migrated to the new node in one click

presentation.v: Hides all chrome; click a node to walk its data flow, arrow keys to step

views.v: Save the current subset and viewport as a named view, switch in one click

flow.v: Packet dots travel along edges — source and direction at a glance

watch.v: Rescans on source changes; new nodes flash green

perf.v: Viewport culling + low-zoom LOD keep hundreds of nodes smooth; dense graphs fall back to an iterative layout

export.v: Bitmap at 2× device pixels, framing the whole graph regardless of the current zoom/pan.
Hand-built vector: bezier edges, per-color arrow markers, striped service nodes — not a DOM screenshot.
A single self-contained file: the SVG plus an inline vanilla-JS viewer with pan/zoom and click-to-highlight. Opens offline, no dependencies.

features.exportNote: Edge flow animation is a host-browser affordance only; it is hidden during PNG export and at far zoom, and never embedded in SVG/HTML exports.

docs.title: Docs

install.comment1: # in any TS / Next.js project root (needs tsconfig.json)
install.watch: # watch sources, push changes to the browser live
install.scan: # print the graph as JSON for scripting
install.comment2: # after a global install, use the cct shorthand (cc is taken by Claude Code)

cliCommands.name: codecarto [map] [dir]
cliCommands.desc: Scan dir (default: cwd) and open the localhost map. map is the default command, so codecarto alone works.

cliCommands.name: codecarto scan [dir]
cliCommands.desc: Scan only; prints a kind/edge summary. With --json, writes the full graph JSON to stdout.

cliCommands.name: codecarto init [dir]
cliCommands.desc: Write a codecarto.config.ts template. Config is entirely optional — zero-config works.

cliOptions.name: -p, --port <port>
cliOptions.desc: Preferred port (default 4870); the next free port is used if taken.

cliOptions.name: -w, --watch
cliOptions.desc: Watch source files and push rescans to the browser live; new nodes flash green.

docs.cliAlias: cct is an alias for codecarto — every command and flag is identical.
docs.configNote: Service rules match by module specifier prefix (e.g. import:@scope/pkg) and stack on top of the built-ins.
docs.mapIntro: A single JSON file at the project root, written with stable key ordering for clean diffs. Commit it. Every entry is keyed by a stable node ID (relative file path or route path), so curation re-applies after a rescan.

mapKeys.v: Per-node curation: label, color, icon, hidden, pinned, groupId.
Manual node positions; absent nodes use the automatic layout.
NamedViews: a node subset (empty = all) plus an optional viewport.

privacy: Scanning runs entirely on your machine.
The server binds to 127.0.0.1 only and validates the Host and Origin headers (DNS-rebinding protection).
The analysis cache goes to node_modules/.cache/codecarto/, not your source tree.
The only file ever written to your project is codecarto.map.json.
